tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text:
{"number":57243,"title":"MPG: Tag methods should not be generated when PATCH operation has no request body","body":"## Description

The current MPG (Management Plane Generator) does not check whether a PATCH operation accepts a request body before deciding to generate tag methods (`AddTag`/`SetTags`/`RemoveTag`). If a resource has a `tags` property (e.g. inherits from `TrackedResource`) and has a PATCH operation that takes **no request body**, the generator may still produce tag methods — but those methods would be non-functional since there is no way to write updated tags back to the service via the bodyless PATCH.

## Current Behavior

In `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()` ([ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409)), the tag generation gate is:

```csharp
if (HasTags() && _readMethod is not null && updateMethodProvider is not null)
```

`updateMethodProvider` is set to non-null whenever a `ResourceOperationKind.Create` (PUT) or `ResourceOperationKind.Update` (PATCH) method exists (line 382), **regardless of whether the PATCH operation accepts a body parameter**.

The fallback path in `BaseTagMethodProvider.BuildElseStatements()` ([BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/TagMethodProviders/BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228)) then does:

```csharp
var updateParam = _updateMethodProvider.Signature.Parameters
    .Where(p => !p.Type.Equals(typeof(WaitUntil))).First();
```

This assumes a body parameter exists. For a bodyless PATCH, this would either crash or produce nonsensical code.

## Expected Behavior

The generator should **skip tag method generation** when:
- The resource has no PUT (`CreateOrUpdate`) operation, **and**
- The PATCH (`Update`) operation has **no request body** (i.e. no parameter to carry tag changes)

## Evidence from Existing SDKs

Analysis of the existing generated SDKs shows this pattern clearly. Among the 34 `TrackedResourceData` resources without tag methods in the current repo:

| Category | Count |
|---|---|
| No PATCH at all | 16 |
| PATCH exists but has no body | 16 |
| Has full CRUD (anomaly, AppService only) | 2 |

**None** of the 34 have a PATCH with a body. Resources with bodyless PATCHes include:
- `ProviderMonitorSettingResource` (ProviderHub) — `UpdateAsync(CancellationToken)`
- `AppServiceCertificateOrderResource` (CertificateRegistration)
- `CosmosDBFleetResource` (CosmosDB)
- `LabPlanResource` / `LabResource` (LabServices)
- And 11 others

In contrast, **all 568** `TrackedResourceData` resources that correctly have tag methods also have either a PUT or a PATCH **with a body parameter**.

## Suggested Fix

In `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()`, add a check that the update method has a meaningful body parameter before generating tag methods. For example, in `PopulateUpdateClient()` or at the tag generation gate, verify that the PATCH method's input model contains parameters beyond `WaitUntil` and `CancellationToken`.","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57243","user":{"login":"haiyuazhang","id":1924967,"profile_url":"https://github.com/haiyuazhang","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4"},"author_association":"MEMBER","reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"created_at":"2026-03-19T06:00:05Z","updated_at":"2026-03-19T06:00:05Z"}

The current MPG (Management Plane Generator) does not check whether a PATCH operation accepts a request body before deciding to generate tag methods (`AddTag`/`SetTags`/`RemoveTag`). If a resource has a `tags` property (e.g. inherits from `TrackedResource`) and has a PATCH operation that takes **no request body**, the generator may still produce tag methods — but those methods would be non-functional since there is no way to write updated tags back to the service via the bodyless PATCH.

## Current Behavior

In `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()` ([ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409)), the tag generation gate is:


`updateMethodProvider` is set to non-null whenever a `ResourceOperationKind.Create` (PUT) or `ResourceOperationKind.Update` (PATCH) method exists (line 382), **regardless of whether the PATCH operation accepts a body parameter**.

The fallback path in `BaseTagMethodProvider.BuildElseStatements()` ([BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/TagMethodProviders/BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228)) then does:

```csharp
var updateParam = _updateMethodProvider.Signature.Parameters
    .Where(p => !p.Type.Equals(typeof(WaitUntil))).First();
```

This assumes a body parameter exists. For a bodyless PATCH, this would either crash or produce nonsensical code.

## Expected Behavior

The generator should **skip tag method generation** when:
- The resource has no PUT (`CreateOrUpdate`) operation, **and**
- The PATCH (`Update`) operation has **no request body** (i.e. no parameter to carry tag changes)

## Evidence from Existing SDKs

Analysis of the existing generated SDKs shows this pattern clearly. Among the 34 `TrackedResourceData` resources without tag methods in the current repo:

| Category | Count |
|---|---|
| No PATCH at all | 16 |
| PATCH exists but has no body | 16 |
| Has full CRUD (anomaly, AppService only) | 2 |

**None** of the 34 have a PATCH with a body. Resources with bodyless PATCHes include:
- `ProviderMonitorSettingResource` (ProviderHub) — `UpdateAsync(CancellationToken)`
- `AppServiceCertificateOrderResource` (CertificateRegistration)
- `CosmosDBFleetResource` (CosmosDB)
- `LabPlanResource` / `LabResource` (LabServices)
- And 11 others

In contrast, **all 568** `TrackedResourceData` resources that correctly have tag methods also have either a PUT or a PATCH **with a body parameter**.

## Suggested Fix

In `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()`, add a check that the update method has a meaningful body parameter before generating tag methods. For example, in `PopulateUpdateClient()` or at the tag generation gate, verify that the PATCH method's input model contains parameters beyond `WaitUntil` and `CancellationToken`.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"haiyuazhang","id":1924967,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE5MjQ5Njc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/haiyuazhang","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/subscriptions"},"comments":0,"created_at":"2026-03-19T06:00:05Z","updated_at":"2026-03-19T06:00:05Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57243","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57243","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57243/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57243/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57243/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57243/reactions"},"node_id":"I_kwDOACyxMM70WaXz"}]}

{"total_count":2,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":641520894,"number":12877,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"Make DataFactoryManagementClient.Deserialization/SerializationSettings static","body":"We have scenarios in which we need to serialize/deserialize json fragments that represent Data Factory entities. We need to do this WITHOUT connecting to a data factory.

Today we have to COPY definition of settings and maintain them on our side. 

Looking at the source code, these are static in nature, unless some extra customization is in custom init method. Even then, maybe a base setting could be made available in a new API surface.

Scenarios
- Deserialize linkedservice that is passed as parameter by data factory to batch as part of Custom activity execution
- Serialize/Deserialize json files used for XXX-AzDataFactoryYYY comandlets that are used to deploy data factory artifacts.

The two properties in question:
- https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/datafactory/Microsoft.Azure.Management.DataFactory/src/Generated/DataFactoryManagementClient.cs#L38
- https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/datafactory/Microsoft.Azure.Management.DataFactory/src/Generated/DataFactoryManagementClient.cs#L43","author_association":"NONE","user":{"login":"mlugosan","id":12767034,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyNzY3MDM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/12767034?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/mlugosan","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlugosan","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlugosan/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlugosan/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlugosan/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlugosan/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlugosan/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlugosan/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlugosan/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlugosan/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mlugosan/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1178448111,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Mgmt","name":"Mgmt","color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is related to a management package.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ4MTEx"},{"id":1353316183,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/customer-reported","name":"customer-reported","color":"3800e0","description":"Issues that are reported by GitHub users external to the Azure organization.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzUzMzE2MTgz"},{"id":1369687945,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/question","name":"question","color":"eaa875","description":"The issue doesn't require a change to the product in order to be resolved. Most issues start as that","default":true,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzY5Njg3OTQ1"},{"id":1369688296,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/feature-request","name":"feature-request","color":"eaa875","description":"This issue requires a new behavior in the product in order be resolved.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzY5Njg4Mjk2"},{"id":1508411177,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/CodeGen","name":"CodeGen","color":"e99695","description":"Issues that relate to code generation","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxNTA4NDExMTc3"},{"id":1936956638,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/needs-team-attention","name":"needs-team-attention","color":"3BA0F8","description":"Workflow: This issue needs attention from Azure service team or SDK team","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxOTM2OTU2NjM4"}],"comments":2,"closed_at":"2020-07-20T05:45:38Z","created_at":"2020-06-18T20:13:20Z","updated_at":"2023-03-28T17:45:24Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/12877","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/12877","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/12877/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/12877/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/12877/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/12877/reactions"},"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWU2NDE1MjA4OTQ="},{"id":547033035,"number":9381,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"[BUG]ClientRuntime 2.3.18 is not compatible with Newtonsoft.Json 12.0.3","body":"**Describe the bug**
Hi. We recently upgraded Newtonsoft.Json to 12.0.3 from 11.0.2 and started getting this error:

Error invoking operation: Exception has been thrown by the target of an invocation.System.Reflection.TargetInvocationException: Exception has been thrown by the target of an invocation. ---> System.TypeLoadException: Method 'get_SerializationSettings' in type 'Microsoft.Azure.Management.EventGrid.EventGridManagementClient' from assembly 'Microsoft.Azure.Management.EventGrid, Version=0.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=null' does not have an implementation.

***Exception or Stack Trace***

Error invoking operation: Exception has been thrown by the target of an invocation.System.Reflection.TargetInvocationException: Exception has been thrown by the target of an invocation. ---> System.TypeLoadException: Method 'get_SerializationSettings' in type 'Microsoft.Azure.Management.EventGrid.EventGridManagementClient' from assembly 'Microsoft.Azure.Management.EventGrid, Version=0.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=null' does not have an implementation.

***Code Snippet***
Here is a sample implementation of my TestClient:

```
public class EventGridManagementClient:  ServiceClient<TestClient>, IAzureClient {
    public JsonSerializerSettings SerializationSettings { get; private set; }
    public EventGridManagementClient() {
        SerializationSettings = new JsonSerializerSettings();
    }

}
```

**Expected behavior**
It shouldn't crash.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Setup (please complete the following information):**
.Net Framework 4.6.1
- I am using these AutoRest packages:
Microsoft.Rest.ClientRuntime - 2.3.18
Microsoft.Rest.ClientRuntime.Azure - 3.3.18
Microsoft.Rest.ClientRuntime.Azure.Authentication - 2.3.6

","author_association":"NONE","user":{"login":"batrived","id":37561710,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3NTYxNzEw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37561710?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/batrived","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/batrived","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/batrived/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/batrived/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/batrived/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/batrived/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/batrived/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/batrived/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/batrived/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/batrived/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/batrived/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1069434707,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Service%20Attention","name":"Service arguments.query: repo:Azure/azure-sdk-for-net is:issue MPG generator tag methods management

content.text:
{"total_count":2,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4099515891,"number":57243,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"MPG: Tag methods should not be generated when PATCH operation has no request body","body":"## Description

The current MPG (Management Plane Generator) does not check whether a PATCH operation accepts a request body before deciding to generate tag methods (`AddTag`/`SetTags`/`RemoveTag`). If a resource has a `tags` property (e.g. inherits from `TrackedResource`) and has a PATCH operation that takes **no request body**, the generator may still produce tag methods — but those methods would be non-functional since there is no way to write updated tags back to the service via the bodyless PATCH.

## Current Behavior

In `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()` ([ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409)), the tag generation gate is:

```csharp
if (HasTags() && _readMethod is not null && updateMethodProvider is not null)
```

`updateMethodProvider` is set to non-null whenever a `ResourceOperationKind.Create` (PUT) or `ResourceOperationKind.Update` (PATCH) method exists (line 382), **regardless of whether the PATCH operation accepts a body parameter**.

The fallback path in `BaseTagMethodProvider.BuildElseStatements()` ([BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/TagMethodProviders/BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228)) then does:

```csharp
var updateParam = _updateMethodProvider.Signature.Parameters
    .Where(p => !p.Type.Equals(typeof(WaitUntil))).First();
```

This assumes a body parameter exists. For a bodyless PATCH, this would either crash or produce nonsensical code.

## Expected Behavior

The generator should **skip tag method generation** when:
- The resource has no PUT (`CreateOrUpdate`) operation, **and**
- The PATCH (`Update`) operation has **no request body** (i.e. no parameter to carry tag changes)

## Evidence from Existing SDKs

Analysis of the existing generated SDKs shows this pattern clearly. Among the 34 `TrackedResourceData` resources without tag methods in the current repo:

| Category | Count |
|---|---|
| No PATCH at all | 16 |
| PATCH exists but has no body | 16 |
| Has full CRUD (anomaly, AppService only) | 2 |

**None** of the 34 have a PATCH with a body. Resources with bodyless PATCHes include:
- `ProviderMonitorSettingResource` (ProviderHub) — `UpdateAsync(CancellationToken)`
- `AppServiceCertificateOrderResource` (CertificateRegistration)
- `CosmosDBFleetResource` (CosmosDB)
- `LabPlanResource` / `LabResource` (LabServices)
- And 11 others

In contrast, **all 568** `TrackedResourceData` resources that correctly have tag methods also have either a PUT or a PATCH **with a body parameter**.

## Suggested Fix

In `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()`, add a check that the update method has a meaningful body parameter before generating tag methods. For example, in `PopulateUpdateClient()` or at the tag generation gate, verify that the PATCH method's input model contains parameters beyond `WaitUntil` and `CancellationToken`.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"haiyuazhang","id":1924967,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE5MjQ5Njc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/haiyuazhang","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/subscriptions"},"assignee":{"login":"Copilot","id":198982749,"node_id":"BOT_kgDOC9w8XQ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/1143301?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/apps/copilot-swe-agent","gravatar_id":"","type":"Bot","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/Copilot","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Copilot/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Copilot/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Copilot/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Copilot/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Copilot/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Copilot/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Copilot/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Copilot/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Copilot/subscriptions"},"comments":0,"created_at":"2026-03-19T06:00:05Z","updated_at":"2026-03-19T06:02:53Z arguments.message: No action needed: Issue #57243 already has assignees (haiyuazhang and Copilot). Precondition check failed — issue has an assignee, so automated triage is skipped to avoid interfering with existing assignment.
